step_1.1.task: Init Vite React App & FastAPI struct
step_1.1.note: Initialized folders; added root package.json with workspaces and fixed Vercel tsc build script error.

step_1.2.note: Tailwind setup; updated typography contrast using light CSS variables and Chart.js options

step_2.2.note: Updated ManageEngine station list mock data with a responsive 5-column grid layout.

step_3.2.note: pool_size=10, max_overflow=20, aiosqlite for local dev

step_4.1.task: Teams SDK Frontend Init & Mock Auth
step_4.1.note: useTeamsAuth.js hook created with local/prod toggle

step_4.2.task: FastAPI Teams Token Validator & OBO Flow
step_4.2.note: JWKS-based JWT validation + MSAL OBO exchange implemented. get_current_user dependency wired into all routes. /api/v1/me verified 200 OK.

step_5.1.task: Live Microsoft Graph API Integration (M365 Backend)
step_5.1.note: Full M365 live Graph integration: .env populated with Azure credentials; CORS tightened with Vercel origin + PATCH/If-Match; routers/m365.py rewritten with live Graph helpers, per-user cache (m365:{oid}:{section}), graceful section-level errors, PATCH task endpoint with ETag/409 handling; manageengine.py /all updated to full contract shape.

step_5.2.task: Connect UI Hooks to Live FastAPI Endpoints (Frontend Wiring)
step_5.2.note: Created src/lib/apiClient.js, hooks/useM365.js, hooks/useManageEngine.js. Rewired App.jsx, M365Widgets, ManageEngine, KpiRow, Header to consume live data. Fixed backend from_cache bug. Build: 183 modules, 0 errors.

step_5.3.note: Root cause: (1) FastAPI backend was NOT running — Vite proxy had nothing to forward to, returning 502; (2) Multiple stale Vite dev servers (PIDs on 3000/3001/3002) caused the frontend to land on port 3002 where the wrong proxy was active. Fixes: (a) Created frontend/.env (VITE_API_BASE_URL= empty, VITE_ENV=local for proxy-based local dev); (b) Created frontend/.env.production (VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://it-cockpit-api.up.railway.app for Vercel build); (c) vite.config.js rewritten with strictPort:true (prevents silent port shifting to 3002) and proxy error handler returning 503 JSON instead of 502; (d) backend/main.py CORS updated: hardcoded Vercel URL https://it-cockpit-frontend.vercel.app, added localhost:3001/3002 defensively, de-duplicated origins list; (e) Killed stale Vite processes on ports 3001/3002; (f) Started FastAPI on port 8000. Verification: /health=200 ok, /api/v1/me=200, /api/v1/manageengine/all=200, /api/v1/m365/all=200. Vite proxy → localhost:8000 → 200 confirmed.

step_5.4.note: Replace mock data in manageengine.py with live parameterized SQL queries via SQLAlchemy asyncio. Requires production DATABASE_URL and confirmed table/column schema.

step_5.5.task: Developer Experience: Combined Start Script & Proxy Hardening
step_5.5.note: Installed concurrently@10. Added npm run dev:all to root package.json — launches Uvicorn (BACKEND, blue) and Vite (FRONTEND, green) concurrently with --kill-others-on-fail. Added proxyTimeout:3000 and req.socket.destroy() to vite.config.js proxy error handler to suppress AggregateError unhandled rejections. EPIPE/ECONNRESET errors silenced; only ECONNREFUSED prints a clean ⚠️ warning.

step_5.7.task: Definitive AggregateError Fix & Graceful ServerConnecting Overlay
step_5.7.note: Two-part fix: (1) vite.config.js — added proxy.removeAllListeners('error') before registering our handler. Vite registers its own internal error listener on the http-proxy instance that re-prints the AggregateError stacktrace regardless of the user-defined handler; removing all listeners first gives us sole ownership and silences Vite's own log line. (2) Graceful UI fallback — created ServerConnecting.jsx overlay (pulsing animated spinner + countdown + 'npm run dev:all' hint) that renders when useManageEngine.backendDown=true. The overlay polls /health every 4 s using setInterval and auto-dismisses by calling handleConnected() → refresh() once the backend responds. useManageEngine hook updated to expose backendDown boolean (true when err.code==='BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE'). App.jsx updated to import ServerConnecting and conditionally render it. No page refresh required for recovery.

step_5.8.task: MSAL Browser Login Fallback for Local Dev Outside Teams
step_5.8.note: Implemented full MSAL browser authentication fallback. Installed @azure/msal-browser + @azure/msal-react in frontend. Created src/lib/msalConfig.js (VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID/TENANT_ID, Graph scopes: Mail.Read/Tasks.Read/Calendars.Read/User.Read). Rewrote useTeamsAuth.js: (1) tries Teams SDK first; (2) if SDK fails/not in Teams, initialises PublicClientApplication singleton, tries acquireTokenSilent from cache, otherwise sets needsMsalLogin=true; (3) msalLogin() callback triggers loginPopup({scopes:GRAPH_SCOPES}). Created MsalLoginScreen.jsx — premium sign-in page with animated rings, Microsoft four-square logo button, spinner during popup, inline error display. Created src/lib/msalConfig.js. App.jsx updated: calls useTeamsAuth() directly to access needsMsalLogin/msalLogin; shows auth-init spinner, MsalLoginScreen gate, then dashboard. apiClient.js updated: apiFetch() accepts authMode param and sets X-Auth-Mode header. useManageEngine/useM365 pass authMode through. Backend: teams_validator.py get_current_user now accepts Request param; new Path 2 — when APP_ENV=local + X-Auth-Mode:msal-direct, decodes JWT without sig verification, extracts real claims, uses token as pass-through Graph token (live M365 data). main.py CORS updated to allow X-Auth-Mode header. frontend/.env updated with VITE_AZURE_CLIENT_ID and VITE_AZURE_TENANT_ID.

step_5.9.task: MSAL Redirect-Only Flow Pivot, Direct Local Tokens & Logging
step_5.9.note: Fully pivoted MSAL browser fallback flow to Redirect-Only with direct local tokens and debug logging support: (1) Removed loginPopup and popup blocker catch blocks entirely to prevent block_nested_popups. (2) Set onClick handler to execute loginRedirect(). (3) Enhanced getMsalInitPromise() and init() useEffect to handle Redirect response, extract accessToken, set active account, and support silent token loads. (4) Updated teams_validator.py to check for APP_ENV=local and bypass OBO entirely. Decodes token claims via best-effort jwt.decode; if token is opaque/encrypted, falls back to generic claims but still passes token directly to Graph. (5) Served hardcoded premium M365 and ManageEngine mock responses if a mock token is detected. (6) Setup uvicorn logger to print detailed statuses and response texts for Graph GET/PATCH calls. (7) Refactored useM365 and useManageEngine hooks to accept token and authMode as arguments from App.jsx, resolving a critical React hook state-isolation bug where multiple useTeamsAuth() instantiations failed to share state. (8) Added verbose console.log statements inside hooks to expose backend payloads, and verified all builds compile with zero errors.

step_5.10.note: Completed deployment prep, repository cleanups, and Vercel serverless configurations: (1) Added gunicorn to requirements.txt for production. (2) Created DEPLOYMENT_ENV_VARS.md guide detailing settings for Vercel and Railway/Render. (3) Added a root .gitignore file to exclude local secrets and Python caches, and untracked them from Git index. (4) Created vercel.json mapping all api routes to api/index.py under @vercel/python builder. (5) Bridged FastAPI app resolution by writing api/index.py to import app directly from backend.main (via 'from backend.main import app') and inserted root/backend paths into sys.path. (6) Created backend/__init__.py as a package marker for Python's module importer. (7) Created root requirements.txt for Vercel builder dependency installs. (8) Created root README.md detailing step-by-step monorepo deployment steps on Vercel. (9) Verified CORS configuration and successfully pushed all updates to main branch.

step_5.11.note: Decoupled frontend and backend architectures for Render.com backend hosting: (1) Removed Vercel serverless configuration files (deleted root 'api/' folder, api/index.py, and vercel.json); (2) Updated apiClient.js to construct absolute URLs utilizing VITE_API_BASE_URL with fallback to http://127.0.0.1:8000; (3) Updated ServerConnecting.jsx to resolve HEALTH_URL to absolute format; (4) Configured VITE_API_BASE_URL placeholder in frontend/.env.production to point to Render production backend URL; (5) Verified root requirements.txt is up-to-date and includes uvicorn/gunicorn; (6) Verified frontend compiles cleanly.

step_6.1.note: ChatbotWidget.jsx created with FAB button, animated chat window, Copilot Studio iframe. Build: 25 modules, 0 errors.
